body.title: Build intent extractor device — predict/validate/patterns API
body.result: IntentExtractorDevice built — predict/validate/patterns API with few-shot learning; devlab.predictions + devlab.validations schema; 28 tests all pass including criterion 5 learning test
body.intention: I intend that a callable intent extractor device exists that accepts predict/validate/patterns calls, stores validated predictions in devlab, and improves its predict accuracy over successive validation rounds.
body.description:
The system has no canonical learning substrate. Every device that learns does it privately and incompatibly. The intent extractor is the shared service all learning passes through.

Three-method API:
  predict(context: str, domain: str) -> {prediction_id, intent: str, confidence: float}
  validate(prediction_id: str | None, actual_outcome: str) -> None
    (prediction_id=None is valid: adds a ground-truth example with no prior prediction)
  patterns(domain: str) -> list[{pattern, confidence, validation_count}]

V1 learning mechanism: store-and-retrieve. Every validate() call persists prediction+outcome pair to devlab.validations. predict() retrieves top-K validated examples for the domain and uses them as few-shot context in a lightweight inference call (Haiku-class). patterns() aggregates validated pairs into recurring intent patterns.

Storage:
  devlab.predictions (id, context, domain, predicted_intent, confidence, created_at)
  devlab.validations (id, prediction_id NULLABLE FK->devlab.predictions, actual_outcome, match bool, created_at)
  prediction_id is nullable by design: Librarian and other post-hoc callers log ground-truth examples without a prior predict() call.

Device home: devices/intent/ (own device — domain-agnostic, not nested in hubert).

Librarian exception built into API: validate(prediction_id=None) is the post-hoc path. Librarian never calls predict().

**Affected files:** devices/intent/__init__.py, devices/intent/device.py, devices/intent/store.py (new); SQL migrations devlab.predictions + devlab.validations; MCP tool registration
**Design rules:** external-state; no SQLite; MCP bus; prediction_id nullable in schema
**Scope boundary:** IN — device + API + devlab storage + V1 few-shot learning + nullable prediction_id schema. OUT — fine-tuning a model; wiring callers (separate tickets); prediction accuracy dashboard.
**Completion criteria:**
  1. predict() returns intent string and prediction_id; verifiable via MCP call
  2. validate() stores to devlab.validations — verifiable via psql row count
  3. validate(prediction_id=None) succeeds and stores a row with null prediction_id
  4. patterns() returns non-empty list after 10+ validations in a domain
  5. LEARNING TEST: measure predict() accuracy on 10 held-out tickets at baseline (0 validations) then again after 20 validation signals in the same domain. Require positive delta (after-20 accuracy > baseline). This is the criterion that proves few-shot retrieval earns its complexity — a static threshold is not sufficient.
